title: Module 1 Project: Course Enrollment Directory
summary: Build a department-to-student enrollment directory through five cumulative multi-table steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Project Overview: Course Enrollment Directory
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will take on the role of a reporting analyst tasked with creating a comprehensive course enrollment directory. This directory will connect departments, courses, enrollments, and students to provide a clear view of who is taking each course and which department offers it. The final deliverable will be a four-table directory that accurately reflects the relationships between these entities. You will build this directory step-by-step, ensuring each join is correctly implemented to maintain data integrity and readability.

quizDraft.title: Connect Courses to Departments
quizDraft.prompt: Join the courses table to the departments table and return the course title along with the department name.
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

quizDraft.title: Add Enrollment Records
quizDraft.prompt: Extend the query to include enrollments so each result row represents one enrollment in a course.
quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id;
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

quizDraft.title: Add Student Details
quizDraft.prompt: Extend the query to include student details such as name and grade level.
quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT courses.title, departments.name, students.name, students.grade_level, enrollments.term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

quizDraft.title: Clarify the Directory Output
quizDraft.prompt: Use exact aliases for department, course, student, grade_level, and term without changing the row grain.
quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT courses.title AS course, departments.name AS department, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id;
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.

quizDraft.title: Sort the Directory for Review
quizDraft.prompt: Order the results by department, course, and student in ascending order for a clear review.
quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id ORDER BY department, course, student;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT departments.name AS department, courses.title AS course, students.name AS student, students.grade_level AS grade_level, enrollments.term AS term FROM courses INNER JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id INNER JOIN enrollments ON courses.id = enrollments.course_id INNER JOIN students ON enrollments.student_id = students.id ORDER BY department ASC, course ASC, student ASC;
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.
